# Milestone 2: Agile Meeting Recording

## Project Name
**CSPB Course Review Platform**

## Team Information
- **Team Number:** 8  
- **Team Name:** Team Infinity

### Team Members (All Present)
- Adam Chathankeo
- Hannah Pfeifer
- Craig Sanders
- Sean Lin

### Meeting Details
- **Meeting Date:** June 25th, 2026  
- **Meeting Duration:** ~32 minutes  
- **Meeting Format:** Zoom (synchronous)  
- **Instructor Invitation:** Instructor was invited in advance to attend if available.

---

## Purpose of This Meeting
This meeting represents a **weekly Agile meeting conducted after a sprint**. The goal was to:
- Synchronize team progress
- Demonstrate sprint outcomes
- Reflect on team process and identify improvements

The meeting followed a standard Agile structure:
1. Weekly Standup  
2. End-of-Sprint Demonstration  
3. Sprint Retrospective  

---

# 1. Weekly Standup (≈ 7 minutes total)

Each team member provided updates addressing:
- What they did last week  
- What they are doing this week  
- Any obstacles  

---

### Craig Sanders
- **Last week:**  
  Collecting all decisions for sprint we just did and put it together in a Powerpoint
- **This week:**  
  Refine contributions for user stories
- **Obstacles:**  
  No major obstacles reported.

---

### Hannah Pfeifer
- **Last week:**  
  Started collaborative Google Doc and added some user stories and comments on the features and prof recommendations
- **This week:**  
  After learning more Flask, start setting up some basic table or HTML so we have something tangible to start with
- **Obstacles:**  
  Learning curve for Flask and HTML, time

---

### Adam Chathankeo
- **Last week:**  
  Took professor's feedback and added comments to Google doc, added user stories on user and admin side to see what the flow would be like for each aspect of course review. Created beginning wireframes to see what feature we want and get an idea of what it could look like.
- **This week:**  
  Confirm concise list of features we want and tasks we need.
- **Obstacles:**  
  Group effort to see want we want and don't want to narrow down MVP.

---

### Sean Lin
- **Last week:**  
  Brainstorming on Google Doc, finished remaining user stories. 
- **This week:**  
  Create document with specifications on features with more detail than high level user stories (Add another tab on Google Doc for this)
- **Obstacles:**  
  Not sure where to get started, possibly start with web page. Need to narrow down tasks and find out how to communicate between front end and back end.

---

# 2. End-of-Sprint Demonstration (≈ 19 minutes)

**Sprint Goal:**  
Establish a shared understanding of project scope, features, roles, and initial technical direction.

**Demonstration Summary:**  
The team demonstrated progress by walking through:
- The agreed-upon project scope and minimum viable product features
- Address Prof. Guinn's recommendations
- Initial wireframe sketches for key pages (Login/Create Account, Dashboard, Submit a Review Form)
- Hash out some more core functionality

**Notes:**  
- The demonstration focused on **concepts and visible design artifacts**, not code or database implementations.
- The walkthrough was presented at a high level, appropriate for a non-technical client audience.

---

# 3. Sprint Retrospective (≈ 5 minutes)

### What Went Well
- Collaborated well after last team meeting
- Good intuition on how to communicate
- Created Google Doc for "real-time" collaboration
- Early agreement on scope and expectations

### What Didn’t Work
- Could be more structured. Not sure what specifically each person should do
- Trust each other to do a certain task rather than all working on small pieces of the same thing

### How the Team Can Improve
- Utilize Trello board more so we have clear and focused tasks
- Define features and functionality more so we have a clear direction and structure
- Continue improving familiarity with Agile practices
- Continue to add comments to Doc when more ideas come up

---

## Action Items for Next Sprint
- Finalize formal user stories for all roles
- Resolve open questions
- Confirm DB hosting approach
- Initialize Flask project structure
- Create SQLite Schema
- Build account login/registration pages
- Implement session management with Flask login
- Wireframe main pages (ex: what exactly each button does, user flow)
- Prepare materials for upcoming milestones
- Maintain weekly Agile meetings following the same structure

---

## Reflection
This Agile meeting helped the team align on progress and process early in the project. By following a structured standup, demonstration, and retrospective format, the team established a strong foundation for iterative development throughout the semester.
